solute.description: This molecule, N-tert-butylacrylamide, consists of a vinyl group conjugated to a carbonyl, forming an acryloyl moiety linked to a tert-butyl group via a secondary amide. The core structure is a short, branched aliphatic chain characterized by the significant steric bulk of the tert-butyl substituent attached directly to the nitrogen atom. It is a polar molecule featuring a secondary amide functional group that provides both hydrogen bond donor (N-H) and acceptor (C=O) sites. The structure is distinctive for its reactive terminal alkene and the localized density of methyl groups, which creates a compact but sterically hindered hydrophobic region adjacent to the polar amide bond.

solvent.description: This molecule, dichloromethane, consists of a single central carbon atom covalently bonded to two chlorine atoms and two hydrogen atoms in a tetrahedral geometry. It is classified as a geminal dihalide, characterized by the presence of two electronegative chlorine substituents attached to the same saturated carbon center. Despite its small molecular size and lack of hydrogen bond donors, the molecule possesses a permanent dipole moment due to the polar C-Cl bonds. Its distinctive character arises from the concentration of significant electronegativity and atomic mass on a minimal one-carbon framework.
